content.problemStatement: Write a function named **calculatePower** that takes two integer, **base** and **exponent** respectively, and returns the result of raising **base** to the power of **exponent**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two space-separated integers: base and exponent.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the value of base raised to the power exponent on a separate line.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the concept of exponentiation by squaring, also known as fast power. This method reduces the time complexity of calculating the power of a number from O(n) to O(log n).
solution.timeComplexity: O(log exponent) per test case, O(T * log exponent) total.

code.Python3:
import sys

def fast_power(base, exponent):
    result = 1
    while exponent:
        if exponent & 1: result *= base
        base *= base
        exponent >>= 1
    return result

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
T = int(data[0])
for i in range(T):
    base, exponent = map(int, data[i+1].split())
    print(fast_power(base, exponent))
